body:
**Suggestion:** Directly reading environment variables from `process.env` in code that is meant to run in the browser will throw a ReferenceError because `process` is not defined in the browser; you should guard access to `process.env` and fall back to defaults when it is unavailable. [possible bug]
Severity Level: Major ⚠️ ```mdx - ❌ WebContainer boot helper crashes in plain browser usage. - ⚠️ Browser-only debugging of WebContainer tests becomes impossible. ```
```suggestion const clientId = typeof process !== 'undefined' && process.env ? process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBCONTAINER_CLIENT_ID || 'wc_api_____' : 'wc_api_____'; const scope = typeof process !== 'undefined' && process.env ? process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBCONTAINER_SCOPE || '' : ''; ```
Steps of Reproduction ✅ ```mdx 1. Open the browser test page mentioned in the file header (`__tests__/webcontainer-test-page.html`) so that it loads `__tests__/webcontainer-integration.test.js`. 2. Ensure the page includes the script directly (no bundler/Next.js pipeline that injects a `process` polyfill), so in the browser `typeof process === 'undefined'`. 3. In DevTools console, call `await runWebContainerTests()` which is defined in `__tests__/webcontainer-integration.test.js:294-352`. 4. When `runWebContainerTests` calls `testBoot` at `__tests__/webcontainer-integration.test.js:311`, the function executes `const clientId = process.env...` and `const scope = process.env...` at lines 50-51, causing a `ReferenceError: process is not defined` in the browser. ```
Prompt for AI Agent 🤖 ```mdx This is a comment left during a code review. **Path:** __tests__/webcontainer-integration.test.js **Line:** 50:51 **Comment:** *Possible Bug: Directly reading environment variables from `process.env` in code that is meant to run in the browser will throw a ReferenceError because `process` is not defined in the browser; you should guard access to `process.env` and fall back to defaults when it is unavailable. Validate the correctness of the flagged issue. If correct, How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, implement it and please make it concise. ```
👍 | 👎

```suggestion const clientId = typeof process !== 'undefined' && process.env ? process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBCONTAINER_CLIENT_ID : undefined; if (!clientId) { console.warn('⚠️ NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBCONTAINER_CLIENT_ID not set, using default'); } ```
Steps of Reproduction ✅ ```mdx 1. Open the browser test page referenced in the header (`__tests__/webcontainer-test-page.html`) which includes `__tests__/webcontainer-integration.test.js`. 2. Load it in a plain browser context where `process` is not defined (no bundler-injected `process` polyfill). 3. In the browser DevTools console, execute `await runWebContainerTests()`; this calls the main runner defined at `__tests__/webcontainer-integration.test.js:294-352`. 4. At the start of `runWebContainerTests`, the line `const clientId = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBCONTAINER_CLIENT_ID;` (line 304) executes and immediately throws `ReferenceError: process is not defined`, preventing any WebContainer tests from running. ```
Prompt for AI Agent 🤖 ```mdx This is a comment left during a code review. **Path:** __tests__/webcontainer-integration.test.js **Line:** 304:307 **Comment:** *Possible Bug: In the main runner, accessing `process.env` without checking for `process` will cause a ReferenceError when this helper is executed directly in a browser as suggested by the comments. Validate the correctness of the flagged issue. If correct, How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, implement it and please make it concise. ```

**Suggestion:** The unconditional `module.exports` assignment at the bottom will cause a ReferenceError when this file is loaded directly in a browser (as the comments suggest) because `module` is not defined there. [possible bug]
Severity Level: Major ⚠️ ```mdx - ❌ WebContainer browser integration test script crashes on load. - ⚠️ Developer browser-based test workflow is blocked entirely. ```
```suggestion if (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports) { module.exports = { runWebContainerTests }; } ```
Steps of Reproduction ✅ ```mdx 1. Open the browser test page mentioned in the header comment (`__tests__/webcontainer-test-page.html`), which loads `__tests__/webcontainer-integration.test.js` directly into the browser. 2. In a standard browser environment, verify that `typeof window !== 'undefined'` and `typeof module === 'undefined'`. 3. When the script is loaded, execution reaches the last line `module.exports = { runWebContainerTests };` at `__tests__/webcontainer-integration.test.js:360`. 4. Because `module` is not defined in the browser, this line throws `ReferenceError: module is not defined`, preventing the script from finishing evaluation and breaking the documented "run in browser DevTools console" workflow. ```
Prompt for AI Agent 🤖 ```mdx This is a comment left during a code review. **Path:** __tests__/webcontainer-integration.test.js **Line:** 360:360 **Comment:** *Possible Bug: The unconditional `module.exports` assignment at the bottom will cause a ReferenceError when this file is loaded directly in a browser (as the comments suggest) because `module` is not defined there. Validate the correctness of the flagged issue. If correct, How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, implement it and please make it concise. ```

**Suggestion:** The auto-run handler calls `setTimeout(runAllTests, 1000)` from within an ES module where `runAllTests` is only attached to `window` and not defined as a lexical variable, causing a ReferenceError when the timeout fires and preventing tests from auto-running. [logic error]
Severity Level: Major ⚠️ ```mdx - ⚠️ Auto-run test suite on page load throws ReferenceError. - ⚠️ Tests only start via manual Run All Tests button. ```
```suggestion // Auto-run on load window.addEventListener('load', () => { setTimeout(() => window.runAllTests(), 1000); }); ```
Steps of Reproduction ✅ ```mdx 1. Open `__tests__/webcontainer-test-page.html` in a browser with the module script (lines 103–479) executing successfully (e.g., after fixing the `process` ReferenceError from lines 113–116). 2. The module defines `window.runAllTests = async function() { ... }` at lines 441–467, but does not declare a lexical variable named `runAllTests` in the module scope. 3. At lines 476–479, an auto-run handler is registered: `window.addEventListener('load', () => { setTimeout(runAllTests, 1000); });`. 4. When the `load` event fires and the callback executes, the `setTimeout` call tries to resolve the identifier `runAllTests` lexically, finds no binding in the module scope, and throws `ReferenceError: runAllTests is not defined`, so the test suite never auto-starts (only the manual "Run All Tests" button works via `onclick="runAllTests()"` using `window.runAllTests`). ```
Prompt for AI Agent 🤖 ```mdx This is a comment left during a code review. **Path:** __tests__/webcontainer-test-page.html **Line:** 476:479 **Comment:** *Logic Error: The auto-run handler calls `setTimeout(runAllTests, 1000)` from within an ES module where `runAllTests` is only attached to `window` and not defined as a lexical variable, causing a ReferenceError when the timeout fires and preventing tests from auto-running. Validate the correctness of the flagged issue. If correct, How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, implement it and please make it concise. ```
👍 | 👎

**Suggestion:** The debug log right after auth resolution always logs "Anonymous request (no auth token/session)" even for successfully authenticated users, which is a logic error that will significantly mislead diagnostics and monitoring of real traffic. [logic error]
Severity Level: Major ⚠️ ```mdx - ⚠️ Authenticated traffic misreported as anonymous in chat logs. - ⚠️ Harder to debug auth-specific rate limiting behaviors. ```
```suggestion const authResult = await resolveRequestAuth(request, { allowAnonymous: true }); const userId = authResult.userId || 'anonymous'; if (!authResult.success || !authResult.userId || authResult.userId.startsWith('anon:')) { chatLogger.debug('Anonymous request (no auth token/session)', { requestId, userId }, { authSuccess: authResult.success, }); } else { chatLogger.debug('Authenticated request', { requestId, userId }, { authSuccess: authResult.success, }); } ```
Steps of Reproduction ✅ ```mdx 1. Send an authenticated POST request to `/api/chat` (handled by `POST` in `app/api/chat/route.ts:59-214`) with a valid JWT or session cookie so `resolveRequestAuth` returns `{ success: true, userId: 'user_123', source: 'jwt' }`. 2. At `app/api/chat/route.ts:63-67`, the code computes `const userId = authResult.userId || 'anonymous';` resulting in `userId = 'user_123'`. 3. The next line unconditionally calls `chatLogger.debug('Anonymous request (no auth token/session)', { requestId, userId }, { authSuccess: authResult.success });` regardless of `authResult.success` or `authResult.userId`. 4. Observing the logs from `chatLogger` for this request shows an entry labeled `"Anonymous request (no auth token/session)"` even though `authSuccess` is `true` and `userId` is a real user, making telemetry and diagnostics misclassify all traffic as anonymous. ```
Prompt for AI Agent 🤖 ```mdx This is a comment left during a code review. **Path:** app/api/chat/route.ts **Line:** 63:68 **Comment:** *Logic Error: The debug log right after auth resolution always logs "Anonymous request (no auth token/session)" even for successfully authenticated users, which is a logic error that will significantly mislead diagnostics and monitoring of real traffic. Validate the correctness of the flagged issue. If correct, How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, implement it and please make it concise. ```
👍 | 👎

**Suggestion:** The sanitization regex for raw `WRITE/PATCH/APPLY_DIFF` heredoc command blocks assumes `<<<` and `>>>` are on lines with no indentation, but the guidance text shows indented forms; as a result, many such command blocks will not be stripped and will leak low-level control syntax into the user-visible assistant message. [logic error]
Severity Level: Major ⚠️ ```mdx - ⚠️ Internal fs command syntax leaked into chat UI. - ⚠️ Users may see confusing APPLY_DIFF scaffolding text. ```
```suggestion // Remove raw WRITE/PATCH/APPLY_DIFF heredoc command blocks that leak into visible output next = next.replace(/(?:^|\n)\s*(WRITE|PATCH|APPLY_DIFF)\s+[^\n]+\n\s*<<<\s*\n[\s\S]*?\n\s*>>>(?=\n|$)/g, '\n'); next = next.replace(/(?:^|\n)\s*DELETE\s+[^\n]+(?=\n|$)/g, '\n'); ```
Steps of Reproduction ✅ ```mdx 1. Start the app and call POST `/api/chat` so `POST` in `app/api/chat/route.ts:59-214` handles the request and obtains a model response via `priorityRequestRouter.route`. 2. Have the LLM respond with an `fs-actions` block following the guidance defined in `appendFilesystemContextMessages` at `app/api/chat/route.ts:1036-1158`, for example: ```text ```fs-actions APPLY_DIFF src/utils.ts <<< old code === new code >>> ``` ``` where the `<<<` and `>>>` lines are indented with two spaces. 3. The router response is processed by `unifiedResponseHandler.processResponse`, and its `content` flows into `sanitizeAssistantDisplayContent` at `app/api/chat/route.ts:808-829`, which runs the current regex `\n<<<\n` that only matches unindented `<<<` and therefore does not strip the above indented heredoc block. 4. The resulting `sanitizedResponseContent` still contains the low-level `WRITE/PATCH/APPLY_DIFF` control syntax, and `buildClientVisibleUnifiedResponse` at `app/api/chat/route.ts:831-839` returns it to the client, so the UI shows these internal command blocks instead of clean assistant text. ```
Prompt for AI Agent 🤖 ```mdx This is a comment left during a code review. **Path:** app/api/chat/route.ts **Line:** 820:822 **Comment:** *Logic Error: The sanitization regex for raw `WRITE/PATCH/APPLY_DIFF` heredoc command blocks assumes `<<<` and `>>>` are on lines with no indentation, but the guidance text shows indented forms; as a result, many such command blocks will not be stripped and will leak low-level control syntax into the user-visible assistant message. Validate the correctness of the flagged issue. If correct, How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, implement it and please make it concise. ```
👍 | 👎

**Suggestion:** The component uses a `BarChart3` icon in JSX but only imports `ExternalLink`, `RefreshCw`, `BarChart2`, and `Monitor` from `lucide-react`, so `BarChart3` will be undefined at runtime and cause a ReferenceError when the component renders; import `BarChart3` alongside the other icons. [type error]
Severity Level: Critical 🚨 ```mdx - ❌ Observable notebook embed plugin crashes on first render. - ❌ Any page using this plugin can fail entirely. ```
```suggestion import { ExternalLink, RefreshCw, BarChart2, BarChart3, Monitor } from "lucide-react"; ```
Steps of Reproduction ✅ ```mdx 1. Render the default export `ObservableEmbedPlugin` from `components/plugins/observable-embed-plugin.tsx` (component declared at line 30) within any React tree (e.g., as ``). 2. On initial render, state `isLoading` is `false` by default (initialized at line 33: `const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false);`), so the button in the form at lines 151–163 enters the `!isLoading` branch. 3. React attempts to render the JSX at lines 159–162 where the ternary uses `` in the non-loading state. 4. Because `BarChart3` is never imported (only `ExternalLink, RefreshCw, BarChart2, Monitor` are imported from `"lucide-react"` at line 16), the JavaScript runtime throws `ReferenceError: BarChart3 is not defined` when evaluating that JSX, causing the component (and likely its parent React tree) to fail rendering. ```
Prompt for AI Agent 🤖 ```mdx This is a comment left during a code review. **Path:** components/plugins/observable-embed-plugin.tsx **Line:** 16:16 **Comment:** *Type Error: The component uses a `BarChart3` icon in JSX but only imports `ExternalLink`, `RefreshCw`, `BarChart2`, and `Monitor` from `lucide-react`, so `BarChart3` will be undefined at runtime and cause a ReferenceError when the component renders; import `BarChart3` alongside the other icons. Validate the correctness of the flagged issue. If correct, How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, implement it and please make it concise. ```
👍 | 👎

**Suggestion:** The dev server command is split into tokens but only the first token (`cmd`) is passed to `executeCommand`, so multi-word commands like "npm run dev" run as plain "npm" with no arguments and the preview server never starts. [logic error]
Severity Level: Critical 🚨 ```mdx - ❌ VercelPreviewService.startPreview runs command without its arguments. - ❌ Preview startup fails; waitForPort times out for sandboxes. ```
```suggestion // Start dev server in background try { // Start command (detached so it keeps running) const fullCommand = args.length > 0 ? `${command} ${args.join(' ')}` : command await sandbox.executeCommand(fullCommand, cwd) ```
Steps of Reproduction ✅ ```mdx 1. In `lib/sandbox/services/vercel-preview-service.ts:49-136`, instantiate `VercelPreviewService` or use the exported singleton `vercelPreviewService` at line 280. 2. Call `startPreview` (line 62) with a multi-word command, e.g. omit `command` so the default `'npm run dev'` (line 66) is used, or explicitly pass `command: 'npm run dev'` and a `port` (e.g. 3000). 3. Inside `startPreview`, at lines 90-92 the command is split, but at line 97 the service calls `sandbox.executeCommand(cmd, cwd)` passing only `'npm'` as the command and discarding all arguments (`'run dev'` and any extra `args`). 4. Because the dev server command is never actually invoked with its required arguments, no process binds to the requested port; the subsequent `waitForPort` call at lines 99-101 eventually throws at line 261 (`Port ${port} did not become ready...`), and the preview URL acquisition via `sandbox.getPreviewLink(port)` (line 103) is never reached. ```
Prompt for AI Agent 🤖 ```mdx This is a comment left during a code review. **Path:** lib/sandbox/services/vercel-preview-service.ts **Line:** 90:97 **Comment:** *Logic Error: The dev server command is split into tokens but only the first token (`cmd`) is passed to `executeCommand`, so multi-word commands like "npm run dev" run as plain "npm" with no arguments and the preview server never starts. Validate the correctness of the flagged issue. If correct, How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, implement it and please make it concise. ```
👍 | 👎

**Suggestion:** User-supplied glob patterns are converted directly into regular expressions without guarding against invalid regex syntax, so a malformed pattern (for example with an unmatched backslash) will cause `new RegExp(...)` to throw and crash context pack generation. [possible bug]
Severity Level: Major ⚠️ ```mdx - ❌ Context pack generation crashes on malformed glob patterns. - ⚠️ VFS context pack tools become unusable for that request. ```
```suggestion /** * Simple glob pattern matching (supports * and **) */ private matchGlob(path: string, pattern: string): boolean { // Convert glob pattern to regex let regexPattern = pattern .replace(/\./g, '\\.') .replace(/\*\*/g, '.*') .replace(/\*/g, '[^/]*') .replace(/\?/g, '.'); regexPattern = `^${regexPattern}$`; try { const regex = new RegExp(regexPattern); return regex.test(path); } catch { // If the pattern results in an invalid RegExp, treat it as non-matching return false; } } ```
Steps of Reproduction ✅ ```mdx 1. In `lib/virtual-filesystem/context-pack-service.ts:103-140`, call `contextPackService.generateContextPack('owner-1', '/', { includePatterns: ['[invalid'] })` from a test or script. 2. `generateContextPack()` calls `collectFiles()` at `context-pack-service.ts:196-210`, which calls `collectFilesRecursive()` at `context-pack-service.ts:212-288`. 3. Inside `collectFilesRecursive()`, `filterEntries()` at `context-pack-service.ts:495-519` is invoked, which in turn calls `matchesPatterns()` and then `matchGlob()` at `context-pack-service.ts:537-549` with `pattern='[invalid'`. 4. `matchGlob()` builds `regexPattern='^[invalid$'` and executes `new RegExp(regexPattern)`, which throws `SyntaxError: Invalid regular expression: Unterminated character class`, causing `generateContextPack()` to reject/throw and fail context pack generation. ```
Prompt for AI Agent 🤖 ```mdx This is a comment left during a code review. **Path:** lib/virtual-filesystem/context-pack-service.ts **Line:** 537:549 **Comment:** *Possible Bug: User-supplied glob patterns are converted directly into regular expressions without guarding against invalid regex syntax, so a malformed pattern (for example with an unmatched backslash) will cause `new RegExp(...)` to throw and crash context pack generation. Validate the correctness of the flagged issue. If correct, How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, implement it and please make it concise. ```
👍 | 👎

**Suggestion:** In the sync endpoint, when `direction` is `'bidirectional'` the `result` object returned by `agentFSBridge.syncBidirectional` does not have a `success` property, so `result.success` is always `undefined` and the API response lacks a meaningful success flag; instead, you should compute success from `toSandbox.success` and `fromSandbox.success` and return that. [logic error]
Severity Level: Major ⚠️ ```mdx - ⚠️ Bidirectional sync response omits success flag for clients. - ⚠️ Clients must infer success from nested results manually. ```
```suggestion const success = direction === 'bidirectional' ? result.toSandbox.success && result.fromSandbox.success : result.success; return NextResponse.json({ success, data: result, }); ```
Steps of Reproduction ✅ ```mdx 1. Start the Next.js dev server for this repo so API routes under `app/api` are available. 2. Create or obtain a V2 agent session by calling `POST /api/agent/v2/session` as documented in `V2_IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md:83-104`; note the returned `data.sessionId`. 3. Call the sync endpoint `POST /api/agent/v2/sync` (documented in `V2_IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md:133-148`) with a JSON body like: ```json { "sessionId": "session-id-from-step-2", "direction": "bidirectional", "includePatterns": ["*.ts"], "excludePatterns": ["node_modules/**"] } ``` This hits `app/api/agent/v2/sync/route.ts:28-82`, where `direction === 'bidirectional'` so `result` is assigned from `agentFSBridge.syncBidirectional(userId, resolvedConversationId, {...})` at line 66. 4. Inside `agentFSBridge.syncBidirectional` (`lib/agent/agent-fs-bridge.ts:177-196`), the function returns an object `{ toSandbox: SyncResult; fromSandbox: SyncResult }` which has no top-level `success` property. Control returns to `app/api/agent/v2/sync/route.ts:84-87`, where the response is built as: ```ts return NextResponse.json({ success: result.success, data: result, }); ``` Because `result.success` is `undefined` for the bidirectional case, the serialized JSON response contains no `success` field at all (Next.js omits properties with `undefined`), so clients calling `/api/agent/v2/sync` in bidirectional mode cannot rely on the top-level `success` flag. ```

**Suggestion:** The workspace path composed from `userId` and `conversationId` is interpolated directly into a shell command (`mkdir -p ${workspacePath}`) without any escaping, which can both break directory creation for IDs containing spaces or shell metacharacters and open a command injection vector if those values are ever attacker-controlled. [security]
Severity Level: Critical 🚨 ```mdx - ❌ V2 session creation can fail with 500 for some IDs. - ❌ Agent V2 `/session` endpoint vulnerable to sandbox command injection. - ⚠️ Breaks workspace setup for conversation IDs containing spaces. ```
```suggestion // Ensure workspace directory exists const safeWorkspacePath = workspacePath.replace(/"/g, '\\"'); await sandbox.executeCommand(`mkdir -p "${safeWorkspacePath}"`); ```
Steps of Reproduction ✅ ```mdx 1. Run the application so that the Next.js API routes are active (including `POST /api/agent/v2/session` implemented in `app/api/agent/v2/session/route.ts:52-120`). 2. Send an HTTP POST request to `/api/agent/v2/session` with a `conversationId` containing shell-significant characters, for example: - URL: `POST /api/agent/v2/session` - Body (JSON): `{"conversationId": "my session $(echo injected)", "mode": "opencode"}` - This hits the `POST` handler in `app/api/agent/v2/session/route.ts:56-84`, where `conversationId` is only validated as `z.string().min(1)` (lines 20-28) and is not sanitized. 3. Inside that handler, the code calls `agentSessionManager.getOrCreateSession(userId, conversationId, { ... })` (lines 77-84), which in turn invokes `createSession(userId, conversationId, config)` in `lib/agent/agent-session-manager.ts:246-321` when no existing session is found. 4. In `createSession`, `workspacePath` is constructed as ``/workspace/users/${userId}/sessions/${conversationId}`` (line 251) and then interpolated directly into a shell command: `await sandbox.executeCommand(\`mkdir -p ${workspacePath}\`);` (lines 283-284). **Suggestion:** The environment variable intended to configure the system prompt for the `opencode` CLI is only applied to the `cat` process in the pipeline (`VAR=... cat ... | opencode ...`), so `opencode` itself never sees `OPENCODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, meaning the model will ignore the configured system prompt and behave incorrectly. Inside `OpencodeV2Provider.runAgentLoop()` (file `lib/sandbox/providers/opencode-v2-provider.ts:99-283`), the method serializes the prompt into `promptFile` (lines 147-161) and then executes OpenCode via `this.sandboxHandle.executeCommand(...)` at lines 167-171, using the shell command: `OPENCODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT='...' cat ${promptFile} | opencode chat --json ...`. 4. In POSIX shells, an inline `VAR=value` assignment before a command only affects that immediate command; in this pipeline `VAR=value` applies to `cat` but not to `opencode`.
